name: Rum-Sauced Pumpkin Ice Cream Pie
recipeInstructions:
1. For the crust, combine crushed crackers, melted margarine, and granulated sugar in a bowl. Mix well.
2. Spread the mixture evenly into a 9-inch pie plate, pressing it onto the bottom and up the sides to form a firm, even crust.
3. Chill for about 1 hour or freeze for 20 minutes, or until firm.
4. Meanwhile, for the rum sauce, mix brown sugar and cornstarch in a small saucepan. Stir in water.
5. Add evaporated milk and corn syrup. Cook over medium heat until thickened and bubbly (mixture may appear curdled).
6. Cook for 2 minutes more. Remove from heat; stir in the 1 tablespoon margarine or butter and the rum.
7. Set aside to cool.
8. In a large mixing bowl, stir together pumpkin and pumpkin pie spice.
9. Fold in ice cream and the thawed whipped topping.
10. Pour half of the pumpkin mixture into the prepared crust.
11. Reserve 2 tablespoons of sauce for the top of the pie.
12. Pour the remaining sauce evenly over the pumpkin layer, marbling slightly.
13. Top with the remaining pumpkin mixture.
14. Drizzle with the reserved sauce.
15. Cover and freeze for at least 4 hours or until firm.
16. To serve, let stand at room temperature for 15 to 30 minutes.
17. Dollop with whipped topping and sprinkle with chopped pecans.
18. Makes 8 servings.
